Just now

“The problem with algorithms is that they reflect the prejudices of their creators.” — Cathy O’Neil
The Recommendation Apocalypse: We’re All Just Numbers in a Machine
You know that moment. It’s 2 AM. You’ve just finished a show that changed your life, and you’re staring at your screen, hungry for more. The algorithm has ONE JOB. It stares back, dead in the digital eyes, and suggests… the same thing you’ve already watched three times.
My friend, you’ve just encountered the existential crisis of the recommendation system. It’s not just annoying. It’s a multi-billion dollar problem that defines our digital existence. And as someone who’s spent years in the trenches of data science, I’m about to pull back the curtain on the chaos happening behind your screen.
Let’s talk about the four horsemen of this digital apocalypse. But unlike the biblical version, these four are riding directly into your user interface.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

The Ghost Town Problem: When New Users Are Just a Blank Slate
You are walking into a library with absolutely no reading history.
